engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Shogun Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Shogun across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Early workhorse diesel powering first-gen Shoguns; robust design but turbo and injector wear common at high mileage.
compatibleVariants: 2.5 TD, 2.5 TD GL, 2.5 TD GLS, 2.5 TD Equippe, 2.5 TD SWB, 2.5 TD LWB
commonFailures: Turbocharger wear and boost loss (80k+ miles)
Injector nozzle coking causing rough idle
Timing belt tensioner failure if not serviced
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D56 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Upgraded 2.8L TD replaced 4D56; improved torque for towing but EGR and injector issues emerged in later units.
compatibleVariants: 2.8 TD, 2.8 TD GL, 2.8 TD GLS, 2.8 TD Equippe, 2.8 TD SWB, 2.8 TD LWB, 2.8 TD Warrior
commonFailures: EGR cooler blockage causing overheating (70k+ miles)
Injector seal leaks leading to fuel dilution
Turbo actuator sticking on V60 models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Diesel
entries.history: Common-rail DI-D flagship; powerful and refined but oil pump bearing weakness drives urgent replacement searches on V70+ models.
compatibleVariants: 3.2 DI-D, 3.2 DI-D GL, 3.2 DI-D GLS, 3.2 DI-D Equippe, 3.2 DI-D SGX, 3.2 DI-D SWB, 3.2 DI-D LWB, 3.2 DI-D Warrior
commonFailures: Oil pump bearing failure causing bottom-end knock (60k-100k miles)
Turbo actuator electrical faults triggering limp mode
High-pressure injector wear affecting fuel economy
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M41 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth naturally aspirated V6; reliable when maintained but timing belt neglect causes catastrophic interference damage.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 V6, 3.0 V6 GL, 3.0 V6 GLS, 3.0 V6 Equippe, 3.0 V6 SWB, 3.0 V6 LWB
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing valve damage if overdue (60k miles)
Balance shaft bearing wear creating rattling noise
Oil consumption from worn piston rings on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G72 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Larger displacement V6 for enhanced towing; shares 6G72 architecture but with reinforced internals for heavy loads.
compatibleVariants: 3.5 V6, 3.5 V6 GLS, 3.5 V6 LWB, 3.5 V6 SWB
commonFailures: Balance shaft bearing failure causing vibration (80k+ miles)
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement
Intake manifold gasket leaks affecting idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G74 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Shogun Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Shogun's rugged reputation masks known weaknesses in its diesel and petrol engines. The 4M41 oil pump failure, 4M40 EGR issues, and timing belt neglect on V6 units dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Shogun 4M41 Oil Pump Failure - Why V70+ Engines Seize Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Factory oil pump bearings wear prematurely under sustained 4x4 load, causing oil pressure loss and bottom-end knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, replace oil pump and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; risk of residual damage if knock already present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded oil pump kit, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; addresses root cause with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: On a Shogun worth £8,000-£15,000, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump kit offers better long-term value than a standalone pump replacement. We recommend the full rebuild if any knocking noise is present.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4M41 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Shogun

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Shogun 4M40 Turbo Actuator Failure - Loss of Power on 2.8 TD
problems.rootCause: Electronic turbo actuator motor fails or sticks, causing limp mode, reduced boost, and poor throttle response.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo actuator motor and recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo core is sound; 12-18 month typical lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Shoguns; includes warranty and full reliability reset

problems.recommendation: If the turbo core shows shaft play or bearing noise, skip the actuator-only fix. A rebuilt 4M40 with upgraded turbo assembly is more cost-effective long-term for workhorse Shoguns.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Shogun Timing Belt Failure - V6 Petrol Catastrophic Risk
problems.rootCause: Timing belt snaps due to age, oil contamination, or tensioner failure; interference design causes valve/piston collision.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on schedule

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V6 with new timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt has snapped; restores reliability with warranty

problems.recommendation: If your V6 Shogun has no service history or is overdue, budget for a preventative belt kit now. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is the only viable recovery option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G72/6G74 V6 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Shogun

problems.group: Mitsubishi Shogun Fuel Injector Failure DI-D
problems.h4: Mitsubishi Shogun Fuel Injector Failure DI-D - Rough Running & Smoke
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common-rail injectors wear internally, causing misfires, excessive smoke, and poor fuel economy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace all four injectors with coding
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is sound; 18-24 month typical lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new injectors and calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Shoguns over 100k miles; resets all wear items with warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Shogun with 100k+ miles, injector replacement alone may trigger further failures. A rebuilt 4M41 with new injectors offers better long-term reliability for rural and towing use.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4M41 Mitsubishi Shogun injector replacement

problems.group: Mitsubishi Shogun EGR Cooler Failure Diesel
problems.h4: Mitsubishi Shogun EGR Cooler Failure Diesel - Overheating & Power Loss
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler cracks internally, allowing coolant into exhaust or vice versa, causing overheating, white smoke, and coolant loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler assembly and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if no head gasket damage; 12-18 month typical lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating has occurred; prevents recurrence with warranty

problems.recommendation: If coolant has entered the cylinders, skip the EGR-only fix. A rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components is the only reliable solution for workhorse Shoguns.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt diesel engine prices for your Mitsubishi Shogun

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Shogun
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Shogun's legendary 4x4 capability depends on a healthy engine. Whether you're facing 4M41 oil pump knock, 4M40 turbo issues, or V6 timing belt neglect, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Shogun specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Shogun Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Shogun and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: The 2.5L 4D56 diesel powered early Shogun generations (V20-V30) as the entry-level workhorse option, offering basic turbocharged reliability for rural and light towing use before the larger 2.8L arrived.

items.title: Mitsubishi Shogun Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates the Shogun range across all generations, with the 4M41 3.2L DI-D driving the highest UK replacement demand due to oil pump failure on V70+ models.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates the Shogun range across all generations, with the 4M41 3.2L DI-D driving the highest UK replacement demand due to oil pump failure on V70+ models.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Shogun Diesel Engine Families
families: 4D56 - Early 2.5L workhorse (V20-V30), turbo wear common at high mileage
4M40 - Core 2.8L TD (V30-V60), EGR and actuator issues on later units
4M41 - Flagship 3.2L DI-D (V70-V90), oil pump bearing weakness drives urgent searches
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and off-road use
4M41 oil pump failure between 60k-100k miles on V70+ models
High UK replacement demand from commercial and rural owners
DPF concerns on post-2007 DI-D units used for short journeys

items.title: Mitsubishi Shogun Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol V6 options offered refined, naturally aspirated power for Shogun buyers prioritising simplicity over diesel torque, though timing belt discipline is critical.

6G74 - 3.5L V6 (V60), enhanced towing capacity with reinforced internals

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Shogun Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Shogun's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The original Shogun arrived with the rugged 4D56 2.5L diesel establishing the model's workhorse reputation.
keyChanges: V20 chassis launched with body-on-frame 4x4 construction
4D56 2.5L turbo diesel introduced as sole engine option
Super Select 4WD system debuted for on-demand 4x4 capability
knownFor: Simple, robust design ideal for rural and agricultural use
Turbo wear and injector coking common above 100k miles
High parts availability due to long production run
Strong residual values for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1999 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Shogun 2.5 TD engine replacement • 4D56 turbo rebuild UK • Shogun diesel injector repair

years.description: The 2.8L 4M40 diesel and 3.0L V6 petrol expanded the Shogun range, offering more power for towing and refined on-road manners.
keyChanges: V30 facelift introduced updated styling and interior
4M40 2.8L TD replaced 4D56 as core diesel option
6G72 3.0L V6 petrol launched for non-diesel buyers
knownFor: 4M40 offered stronger torque for heavy towing applications
EGR cooler blockage emerged as common failure on later 4M40 units
V6 petrol models prized for smoothness but require strict timing belt discipline
Warrior trim became iconic for off-road capability
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2006 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Shogun 4M40 engine replacement • 6G72 timing belt kit UK • Shogun 2.8 TD turbo actuator

years.description: Brief V60 generation bridged pre- and post-DI-D eras, offering both 4M40 diesel and new 6G74 3.5L V6 petrol.
keyChanges: V60 chassis introduced minor styling and equipment updates
6G74 3.5L V6 petrol launched for enhanced towing capacity
Final year for 4M40 2.8L diesel before DI-D transition
knownFor: Short production run makes V60 models relatively rare today
6G74 V6 offered best-in-class towing for petrol Shogun owners
Balance shaft bearing wear noted on high-mileage 6G74 units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2007 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines

years.description: The landmark V70 launch introduced common-rail 4M41 3.2L DI-D diesel, transforming Shogun refinement but introducing the oil pump weakness that dominates today's replacement searches.
keyChanges: All-new V70 platform with improved on-road refinement
Euro 4 emissions compliance achieved with advanced injection
knownFor: 4M41 delivered class-leading torque and refinement for towing
Oil pump bearing weakness emerged as critical failure point (60k-100k miles)
High replacement demand from commercial users experiencing knock
DI-D technology improved fuel economy but added complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2010 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Shogun 4M41 oil pump failure • 3.2 DI-D engine replacement cost • Shogun V70 bottom end rebuild

years.description: V80 facelift refined the DI-D formula with updated styling and equipment while retaining the 4M41 engine and its known oil pump vulnerability.
keyChanges: V80 facelift introduced revised front end and interior updates
4M41 3.2L DI-D carried over with minor calibration tweaks
Enhanced infotainment and safety equipment across range
knownFor: Continued oil pump failure pattern on 4M41 units
Turbo actuator electrical faults became more common on facelift models
Strong residual values maintained due to off-road capability
Warrior trim remained popular for rural and agricultural users
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2013 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Shogun V80 engine replacement • 4M41 turbo actuator fix • Shogun 3.2 DI-D injector rebuild

years.description: The final V90 generation represented the Shogun's swansong, retaining the proven 4M41 DI-D while adding final equipment updates before UK production ended.
keyChanges: V90 final generation launched with updated styling and equipment
4M41 3.2L DI-D carried over as sole engine option
Final UK registrations completed in 2019 before model discontinuation
knownFor: Oil pump failure remains dominant replacement driver on late models
Strong collector interest emerging for low-mileage final-year examples
High demand for rebuilt engines from rural and towing users
Parts availability remains good due to shared Mitsubishi commercial vehicle platforms
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2019 Mitsubishi Shogun replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Shogun V90 engine replacement • 4M41 oil pump upgrade kit • Shogun 2019 last units engine

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Shogun Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £1,400 for early 2.5L TD units to £4,500 for late 3.2L DI-D models, depending on engine code, variant and supplier location. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: 4D56 2.5L TD rebuilt: £1,400-£2,800 supply only
4M41 3.2L DI-D rebuilt: £2,100-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 depending on workshop
Prices include upgraded components addressing known failures
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit the Mitsubishi Shogun?
items.answer: The Shogun used five main engine codes: diesel 4D56 (2.5L), 4M40 (2.8L), 4M41 (3.2L DI-D) and petrol 6G72 (3.0L V6), 6G74 (3.5L V6), fitted across V20-V90 generations from 1991-2019.
keyPoints: 4M41 3.2L DI-D powers all V70-V90 models (2007-2019)
6G72/6G74 V6 petrol options available 1999-2007
Enter your registration above for instant code verification
Compatibility confirmed across SWB/LWB and auto/manual variants
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Shogun engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Shogun 4M41 oil pump failure covered by rebuilt engine warranties?
items.answer: Yes - all rebuilt 4M41 engines supplied through EngineMarket include an upgraded oil pump kit specifically designed to address the factory bearing weakness, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Upgraded oil pump kit included on every rebuilt 4M41 unit
Warranty covers oil pump, bearings and related bottom-end components
Bench testing verifies oil pressure before dispatch
Warranty valid for off-road and towing use when confirmed at order
Full terms provided by chosen supplier before commitment
items.cta: Get quotes for upgraded 4M41 Mitsubishi Shogun engines

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Mitsubishi Shogun?
items.answer: Engine swaps between codes are possible but require specialist knowledge of ECU calibration, wiring harness compatibility and Super Select 4WD system integration - we recommend sticking to the original code for reliability and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: 4M40 to 4M41 swaps require ECU and wiring modifications
Petrol to diesel conversions need emissions compliance validation
Super Select 4WD calibration must match engine management system
Non-original code installations may void warranty coverage
Use our compatibility checker above to confirm original fitment
items.cta: Verify your Mitsubishi Shogun engine compatibility now

items.question: Does Mitsubishi Shogun engine replacement affect towing warranty?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket maintain full towing warranty coverage when the upgraded components address the original failure point and installation is completed by a vetted specialist within our network.
keyPoints: Towing warranty valid when upgraded components are fitted
Installation must be completed by network specialist garage
Documentation of upgrade kit installation required for claims
Warranty covers engine and towing-related stress failures
Confirm towing use at order to ensure correct component specification

items.question: What is the difference between used, reconditioned and rebuilt Mitsubishi Shogun engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with variable condition; reconditioned engines have worn parts replaced and are bench-tested; rebuilt engines undergo comprehensive strip-and-rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failures.
keyPoints: Used: lowest cost, variable mileage, 3-6 month typical warranty
Reconditioned: worn parts replaced, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
Rebuilt: full strip/rebuild with upgrades, best for high-mileage workhorses
All types available through EngineMarket network
Compression test reports provided for used and reconditioned units

items.question: How does Mitsubishi Shogun 4M41 oil pump failure affect replacement choice?
items.answer: The 4M41 oil pump weakness means standalone pump replacement risks residual damage if knocking has occurred - a rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump kit offers better long-term value for Shoguns over 60k miles.
keyPoints: Oil pump failure causes bottom-end knock if not addressed early
Rebuilt engines include upgraded pump kit preventing recurrence
Standalone pump replacement viable only if no knock present
Rebuild option resets all wear items with warranty protection
Cost comparison tool shows rebuilt vs repair economics for your variant
